reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Ilia encurta distância muito bem… mas o Prates tem tamanho, cotovelo, chute de panturrilha e ainda é faixa-preta de j
good: Exceptional share rate (1.7%) — high social discussion potential shows strong shareability for debate-driven content
Strong average watch time (17.2s) indicates solid audience retention past the hook
Bilingual captions (Portuguese/English) expand addressable audience
Expert commentary framing (person on camera analyzing) builds authority and credibility
bad: Ultra-low save rate (3 saves) — analytical tactical content should be bookmarked but isn't
Hook shows talking-head setup instead of immediate fight footage; takes 3+ seconds to reach action
No explicit call-to-action for saves (e.g., 'save this breakdown')
Pacing in first 3 seconds prioritizes setup over visual impact
reels.top_tip: Move fight footage to the first 1-2 seconds. Your shares are strong (people want to debate), so lead with the most contentious tactical moment—elbows, calf kicks, ground game—then cut to the analyst reacting. This preserves discussion value while front-loading the hook.

reels.caption: 🇺🇸 The UFC built the most powerful machine in combat sports. Fighters chase money everywhere… but they still chase the U
good: Exceptional 50.1% engagement rate shows the UFC fighter pay narrative resonates powerfully with viewers
Outstanding 5.1% share rate (100 shares from 1945 views) proves strong shareability—algorithm should favor this
Strong 35.2s average watch time keeps viewers engaged through the complete narrative arc
Clear multi-frame structure: hook (Pax introduction) → explanation → compelling stat ($250M total / 16% to fighters) → comparison with NFL/NBA
bad: Modest 1945 view count suggests the hook frame lacks sufficient visual/textual impact to stop scrolls in the feed
Save rate near 0 (only 1 save) indicates content lands as reactive opinion, not reference material worth bookmarking
Frame 2 'EXPLOITING' text with unexplained names (Ari Emanuel, Mark Shapiro) disrupts narrative flow without context
No explicit call-to-action despite high shareability—missing a direct prompt like 'Tag someone who agrees' or 'Follow for more'
reels.top_tip: Enlarge and bold the text overlay on the hook frame to increase scroll-stop rate; remove or completely rework frame 2 since the 'EXPLOITING' label with unfamiliar figures breaks narrative cohesion and loses engaged viewers

reels.caption: 🇺🇸 The UFC gives visibility. The smartest fighters turn that visibility into a brand before the machine moves to the nex
good: Exceptional 112s average watch time - viewers stay through entire narrative despite modest view count
Strong hook with Dana White as UFC authority figure, immediately establishes credibility
Clear three-act story arc: visibility → brand building → monetization with relatable premise
Visual momentum across frames (interview → fighters → testimonial) maintains pacing
bad: Critically low save rate (2 saves, 0.17%) - content feels consumable but not collectable despite strong engagement
Portuguese text overlay on every frame narrows English-speaking reach during algorithm's crucial first 3 seconds
No visible call-to-action to prompt saves, comments, or follows - leaves engagement potential on table
View count (1193) underperforms the 45.8% engagement rate, suggesting weak initial algorithm distribution
reels.top_tip: Add explicit CTA ('save for your brand strategy' or 'tag a fighter building their empire') in first frame with English-only hook - saves bottleneck algorithm amplification despite strong retention

reels.caption:
🇺🇸 “I created a monster.” 😳🇺🇸 
Years ago, Israel Adesanya said that about Alex Pereira… and now it feels like the monste
good: Exceptional 95.6% engagement rate shows viewers are highly captivated once they click
Strong 58.5s average watch time with effective visual progression from mystery hook to physique reveal to action context
Professional cinematography with dramatic lighting creates immediate scroll-stopping intrigue in hook frame
Bilingual captions (EN/PT) successfully target both English and Portuguese-speaking audiences
bad: Share rate only 1.7% despite high engagement—content isn't spreading virally to reach new viewers
Low absolute reach (495) suggests hook isn't stopping enough initial scrollers despite quality
Missing on-screen text overlays—relying only on captions in description limits mobile viewer comprehension
No clear call-to-action visible—lost opportunity to drive shares ('tag your fight squad,' 'pick: Pereira or Gane')
reels.top_tip: Add on-screen text with explicit CTA ('Tag who you want to see Pereira fight Gane') during peak visual moments and ensure trending UFC/fight audio is being leveraged to unlock algorithmic reach

reels.caption: 🇺🇸 For the first time, attention was leaving the UFC. Ngannou and Jake Paul were pulling massive Google Trends numbers d
good: Hook text 'Dana was crying' creates emotional controversy that stops scrolls—high contrast white text on dark background ensures visibility
Outstanding retention at 9.9s average watch time indicates viewers complete the reel despite the narrative pivot to data visualization
Strong shareability at 1.8% (15 shares) validates sports drama as discussion-worthy content that people want to spread
Face-to-face fighter confrontation visual (Frame 2) delivers expected dramatic confrontation that resonates with fight sports audience
bad: Low reach penetration (61% reach-to-view ratio): high engagement rate isn't translating to algorithmic amplification—hook may not trigger reshare impulse at scale
Minimal saves (1): despite dramatic narrative and credible data proof, content isn't being bookmarked for future reference
Narrative requires caption literacy: the connection between 'Dana's reaction' and the McGregor/Holloway staredown isn't visually self-evident—loses viewers unfamiliar with UFC competitive context
Mid-video momentum dip: pie chart visualization (Frame 3) breaks dramatic pacing when viewers expect emotional reaction or fight footage continuation
reels.top_tip: Clarify the threat in the hook itself: overlay text on Frame 1 connecting Dana's reaction to what's happening (Ngannou/Jake Paul stealing UFC's audience)—this makes the story self-contained before caption reading and converts your strong 79.9% engagement into broader algorithmic reach

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Prospectos, veteranos e lutadores tentando mudar suas carreiras em uma única noite. Quem vai representar o Brasil da
good: Exceptional hook: 37.1% engagement rate shows UFC cage action immediately captures viewer attention in first frame
Strong retention: 6.3s average watch time sustains interest through fighter reveals
Clear typography: high-contrast text (white + red) makes fighter names instantly readable and visually dominant
Bilingual captions expand reach across Portuguese and English-speaking audiences
bad: Broken shareability: only 1 share and 2 saves despite 37% engagement — content entertains but fails to trigger saves/shares
Low absolute reach (729 views) with high engagement suggests algorithm isn't amplifying to broader cold audience
Weak call-to-action: caption asks 'who delivers best?' without explicit prompt to comment, save, or share
Hook targets MMA niche only: leads with fighter action rather than universal stakes — doesn't appeal to casual viewers
reels.top_tip: Lead with stakes narrative ('careers change in one night') before revealing fighters to create urgency and save-worthy moments — current approach entertains fans but lacks the shareability trigger that drives algorithmic growth
